ops.body: Investigation (3-lane workflow, 2026-07-17) — full agent reports at /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-yasyf-Code-cc-steer/7accc04d-b9a3-480a-8b56-a26c2b999eb8/tasks/wbgjjrrbv.output. Diagnosis: today's 13h run (vs 2.4h yesterday, identical recipe) is Tinker-side load variance amplified by our unpipelined client loop. Org contention RULED OUT (zero overlapping runs on the account in 3 days; nearest neighbor ended 69 min before our start); client-change ruled out (rate swung 100→128→522 steps/hr mid-run with no code change; the fast run held ~1264–1332 steps/hr; an isolated same-config baseline run held 834–1309/hr). RANKED FIXES: (1) Pipeline train_lora (cc_steer/retrain/tinker.py:295-306 — currently submit→.result()→submit, 2 blocking round trips × ~3,144 steps ≈ 88% of ~7,113 total calls): submit forward_backward+optim_step together and keep 1-2 steps in flight per the tinker-cookbook supervised/train.py `submit_ahead` deque (docs: naive=3 clock cycles/step, pipelined=1 → up to ~3x); identical artifact and token cost; also hides the SDK's silent default retry (DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES=10, exponential to 10s, worst ~65s/call) which an unpipelined loop pays serially. (2) Concurrent val scoring (tinker.py:370-393): 200 rows × 4 checkpoints = 800 sequential compute_logprobs fired INSIDE the training loop's critical path via on_checkpoint — gather them (SDK allows 2000 in-flight per SamplingClient); same for the 20-row serving diagnostic (score_rows_tinker). (3) checkpoint_fracs 4→2-3 (assets/watcher_recipe.json:8). (4) batch_size 4→16-32 (recipe line 6): docs confirm step time is ~constant per clock cycle regardless of batch and billing is per token, so this cuts cycles ~4-8x at the same cost — but changes training dynamics (LR retune + gate revalidation): run as a C4 sweep arm, not a blind flip. NON-LEVERS: no priority/reserved tier exists; queue time is not billed. COORDINATION (live as of 2026-07-17 ~10:30Z): the cc-steer DEFAULT working copy hosts a running retrain (PID 13260) — do not `uv sync` its .venv (use UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT isolated venvs) and don't edit modules it lazily imports; fable's C5/C3 lanes are working in jj workspaces ../cc-steer-c5 and ../cc-steer-c3 off d0bc7fe6 — take your own workspace; tinker.py also gets touched by the queued C2 dedupe — land pipelining first, C2 rebases.
